reason: TestConcurrentMixedWorkload (as designed per plan.md, per this subtask's own acceptance criteria: disjoint insertOnly/deleteOnly ranges plus a mutate range, oracle-verified final state) does NOT pass self-consistency. This is NOT a test-design defect, scale-tuning issue, or -race/timeout artifact -- it is a genuine, reliably reproducible production correctness bug in Tree.Insert/Tree.Delete's shared propagate logic (engine/btree/insert.go), surfaced by exactly the kind of 3-way (here, minimally 2-way: insert+delete, no lookup needed) concurrent interleaving this capstone subtask exists to hunt for.
step: self-consistency (step 6 of the 9-step workflow) -- go build/go vet/gofmt are clean, but 'go test ./btree/... -race -run TestConcurrentMixedWorkload' is genuinely, correctly red

evidence.original_large_scale_run: 80,000 keys / 160 write goroutines + 40 lookup goroutines under -race hung past a 10-minute timeout (panic: test timed out after 10m0s), with goroutine dump showing heavy contention/no forward progress rather than a clean pass or a clean fast failure.
evidence.bisection: Reducing scale in stages (isolating away lookup goroutines entirely, then away the mutate role entirely, down to just insertOnly + deleteOnly on fully disjoint key ranges) revealed the true root cause: even 20 goroutines (10 insertOnly on [0,1000), 10 deleteOnly on a pre-seeded [1000,2000)), NO mutate, NO lookup goroutines at all, fails RELIABLY (repro'd on essentially every run) in well under 1 second.
evidence.reproduces_without_race: Confirmed with `go test ./btree/... -run TestConcurrentMixedWorkload -count=1` (no -race flag) -- rules out a race-detector timing/instrumentation artifact; this is a genuine logic bug.
evidence.failure_mode_1_explicit_invariant_violat: btree: internal invariant violated: promotedKey "topic0884/page" <= parent.Keys[j-1]="topic1765/page" (j=10, childIDBeingReplaced=11, parentID=3, parent.Keys=[topic0086/page topic1085/page topic0244/page topic1255/page topic1340/page topic0461/page topic1510/page topic1595/page topic1680/page topic1765/page], parent.Children=[1 13 2 4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11]) -- raised by propagate's own internal sanity check in engine/btree/insert.go.
evidence.failure_mode_2_silent_data_loss: An insertOnly-range key (e.g. topic0935/page, topic0670/page, topic0168/page, topic0914/page, topic0965/page across different runs) that Tree.Insert reported inserting successfully (no error returned) is later not found via Lookup, with no error surfaced anywhere -- same *symptom* as 2a.4.2's fix-round-2 silent-data-loss bug.
evidence.hypothesis_not_verified: The error message class (stale positional index j used when inserting a promoted key into a parent's Keys/Children slices during propagate) strongly resembles the promoted-key positional-index bug family from 2a.4.2's fix cycle (GitHub issue #9), which was believed resolved for concurrent-insert-only workloads via 2a.4.2 fix round 3's TryLock+restart-from-root discipline. This finding suggests that discipline does not fully hold once a concurrent Tree.Delete is ALSO mutating the same shared parent/ancestor (e.g. via borrow/merge or spliceOutDegenerateAncestor), invalidating an assumption propagate's positional-index insertion logic makes about the parent's Children/Keys shape being stable enough across the read-decide-write sequence. This is a HYPOTHESIS ONLY -- root-causing insert.go/delete.go is out of this subtask's scope and is explicitly NOT something I (the implementation agent) verified or attempted to fix.

recovery_hint: Per this repository's own established precedent for exactly this bug family (task-2a.4.2's four-round fix cycle for concurrent-insert-only, GitHub issue #9), the recommended next step is a NEW fix-cycle subtask targeting engine/btree/insert.go's propagate (and possibly delete.go's spliceOutDegenerateAncestor/merge paths) to correct the promoted-key positional-index insertion race when Insert and Delete run concurrently against a shared ancestor -- BEFORE task-2a.4.5 can be completed. The minimal repro documented here (20 goroutines, 2,000 keys, no -race needed, fails in <1s) is a fast, reliable regression case for whoever picks up that fix cycle. Once fixed, this subtask (2a.4.5) should be resumed: the test file already written (engine/btree/btree_test.go, TestConcurrentMixedWorkload + TestConcurrentMixedWorkloadForcedLookupDuringDelete) is believed correctly designed per plan.md and does not itself need rework, only re-running against corrected production code.
btree_test_go_state: Contains TestConcurrentMixedWorkload (full 80k-key/200-goroutine design, currently failing due to the production bug above) and TestConcurrentMixedWorkloadForcedLookupDuringDelete (deterministic hook-based test, confirmed PASSING in isolation -- 5/5 clean runs -- since it never exercises concurrent insert+delete against the SAME parent). This file has NOT been committed.
